senses.definition: a drink that is not water

examples.x: Sarah enjoys sipping a hot cup of tea as her morning beverage to start the day.
The café offers a wide selection of beverages, including coffee, tea, smoothies, and freshly squeezed juices.
In many cultures, water is the most common beverage consumed throughout the day.
Jack ordered a refreshing lemonade as his beverage of choice to accompany his meal.
The bartender mixed a variety of alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages to serve at the party.

senses.definition: a cold and non-alcoholic drink that is usually carbonated

examples.x: She grabbed a can of her favorite soft drink from the fridge to cool off on a hot summer day.
He ordered a large soft drink to accompany his meal at the fast-food restaurant.
The convenience store offered a variety of soft drinks, including cola, lemon-lime, and orange soda.
They enjoyed mixing different flavors of soft drinks at the soda fountain.
He liked to sip on a soft drink while watching movies at home, finding it a comforting treat.

senses.definition: (of drinks) without any alcohol

examples.x: She ordered a nonalcoholic cocktail, also known as a mocktail, made with fruit juices and soda water.
He enjoyed a refreshing glass of nonalcoholic beer while socializing with friends at the bar.
The restaurant offered a selection of nonalcoholic beverages, including iced tea and lemonade.
They served nonalcoholic punch at the party for guests who preferred not to drink alcohol.
The grocery store had a section dedicated to nonalcoholic wines and spirits for those abstaining from alcohol.

senses.definition: a sweet fizzy drink that is not alcoholic

examples.x: She ordered a cold soda to quench her thirst after a long day of outdoor activities.
He enjoyed mixing different flavors of soda at the soda fountain, creating his own custom blends.
The convenience store offered a variety of sodas, including cola, root beer, and lemon-lime.
They shared a large pitcher of soda at the diner, refilling their glasses throughout the meal.
She liked to add a scoop of vanilla ice cream to her soda to make a classic ice cream float.

senses.definition: the brand of a sweet and brown drink that has bubbles in it

examples.x: At the movie theater, I always order a large bucket of popcorn and a refreshing Coca-Cola.
She enjoyed sipping on Coca-Cola while relaxing by the pool on a sunny afternoon.
During the road trip, they made a pit stop to grab some snacks, and everyone chose a can of Coca-Cola.

senses.definition: water from underground that contains minerals and gasses, usually bottled and sold

examples.x: She preferred to drink mineral water for its natural purity and mineral content.
He ordered a bottle of sparkling mineral water to accompany his meal at the restaurant.
The grocery store offered a variety of brands of mineral water, ranging from still to sparkling.
They enjoyed a picnic by the lake, sipping on cool bottles of mineral water.
She added a slice of lemon to her mineral water for a hint of citrus flavor.

senses.definition: a cold smooth drink made by mixing milk and ice-cream with fruits, chocolate, etc. as flavor

examples.x: She treated herself to a chocolate milkshake as a special dessert after dinner.
He ordered a strawberry milkshake to go along with his burger and fries at the diner.
They enjoyed sharing a large milkshake with two straws on a hot summer day.
She liked to make her milkshakes at home, experimenting with different flavor combinations.
He craved a milkshake as a nostalgic treat from his childhood, reminding him of carefree days at the soda fountain.

senses.definition: a thick smooth drink made with crushed fruit, ice cream, yogurt, or milk

examples.x: The café offers a variety of refreshing smoothies, including mango-banana and strawberry-kiwi.
Jack enjoys blending up a protein-packed smoothie after his workout at the gym.
The tropical smoothie, made with pineapple, coconut milk, and mango, is a popular choice on hot summer days.
She likes experimenting with different ingredients to create unique smoothie recipes, such as avocado-blueberry and kale-pineapple.

senses.definition: a drink made with water, sugar, and lemon juice

examples.x: On a hot summer day, nothing beats a tall glass of ice-cold lemonade.
The picnic basket was packed with sandwiches and a pitcher of homemade lemonade.
The restaurant offered a variety of flavored lemonades, including raspberry and mint.
After mowing the lawn, he treated himself to a well-deserved glass of fresh lemonade.

senses.definition: a kind of strong black drink made by forcing hot water or steam through coffee

examples.x: Sarah ordered a double shot of espresso to kickstart her morning.
The barista carefully brewed the espresso, producing a rich and intense flavor with a velvety crema on top.
Jack prefers his espresso served in a small cup, savoring the bold taste and aroma.
She enjoys the ritual of making espresso at home, grinding fresh beans and pulling shots with her espresso machine.

senses.definition: a drink made from espresso with steamed milk on top

examples.x: She ordered a latte with almond milk for a creamy and dairy-free coffee option.
He enjoyed sipping a hot latte on a chilly morning, finding comfort in its velvety smoothness.
The café offered a variety of flavored lattes, including vanilla, caramel, and hazelnut.
She liked to sprinkle cinnamon on top of her latte for an extra touch of warmth and flavor.
He savored the rich aroma of his latte as he took his first sip, finding it the perfect start to his day.

senses.definition: a hot or cold beverage made from cocoa powder, milk or water, and sweetener, such as sugar

examples.x: Sarah likes to make a steaming cup of hot cocoa on cold winter nights, topped with marshmallows.
He liked to add a splash of peppermint extract to his cocoa for a festive holiday twist.
The café offered a variety of cocoa options, including classic, dark chocolate, and white chocolate.
They enjoyed sipping cold cocoa on a warm summer day, finding it refreshing and indulgent.
She prepared a steaming cup of cocoa on a snowy afternoon, savoring its comforting warmth.

senses.definition: a drink containing a lot of sugar, caffeine, or other substances that makes one more active

examples.x: After a long night of studying, he reached for an energy drink to help him stay awake.
The athlete drank an energy drink before the race to boost his performance.
Energy drinks often contain caffeine and other stimulants to provide a quick energy boost.
She prefers to drink an energy drink in the morning instead of coffee to kickstart her day.
The athlete avoided energy drinks before the competition.

senses.definition: (of drinks) containing alcohol

examples.x: She enjoyed a glass of alcoholic cider while relaxing on the porch.
The restaurant served a variety of alcoholic cocktails, including martinis and margaritas.
He sampled different alcoholic spirits during the tasting event, appreciating the complexity of flavors.

senses.definition: any drink that can make people intoxicated, such as wine, beer, etc.

examples.x: The legal drinking age for purchasing alcohol in many countries is 21.
Excessive consumption of alcohol can lead to health problems such as liver damage and addiction.
He decided to abstain from alcohol for a month as part of a health challenge.
She enjoys sipping a glass of alcohol in the evening as a way to unwind.
He prefers wine over other types of alcohol.

senses.definition: alcohol or an alcoholic beverage, commonly consumed in social gatherings

examples.x: They decided to meet for drinks after work to celebrate their colleague's promotion.
The sommelier recommended a bold red wine to pair with their steak dinner, ensuring a perfect drink to complement the meal.
They stopped at a bar to grab a quick drink before continuing their sightseeing tour.

senses.definition: to consume alcohol as a habit or for pleasure

examples.x: She doesn't usually drink, but she'll have a cocktail at special occasions.
The doctor advised him to cut back on his drinking and drink more water for better health.
It's important to drink responsibly and know your limits when consuming alcohol at social events.

senses.definition: a drink that is alcoholic and made from different types of grain

examples.x: They tasted different types of beer during the brewery tour.
The fridge was stocked with cans of beer for the party.
During the barbecue, everyone enjoyed a cold and refreshing beer.
The Oktoberfest celebration featured traditional German beers, delighting the attendees.

senses.definition: a type of fizzy wine made originally in France, often drunk to celebrate an event

examples.x: They toasted with glasses of champagne to celebrate their wedding anniversary.
She popped open a bottle of champagne to ring in the New Year with friends.
The wedding reception featured champagne served in crystal flutes for the guests.
They enjoyed a leisurely brunch with mimosas made with champagne and orange juice.
She received a bottle of vintage champagne as a gift for her promotion at work.

senses.definition: a drink that is alcoholic and mostly made from grape juice

examples.x: The winery produced a variety of wines, including a rich merlot.
The friends gathered for a picnic, bringing along a chilled bottle of rosé wine.

senses.definition: a strong alcoholic drink made from grains such as corn and wheat
a strong, clear alcoholic drink made from grain or potatoes, originally from Russia

examples.x: She mixed vodka with tonic water and a squeeze of lime for a refreshing cocktail.
He preferred his vodka served chilled in a martini glass with a twist of lemon.
They toasted the occasion with shots of vodka, raising their glasses to celebrate.
She used vodka as a base for homemade infusions, adding fruits and herbs for flavor.

senses.definition: a very strong alcoholic drink made in Mexico

examples.x: The bartender crafted a classic Margarita using tequila, triple sec, lime juice, and agave syrup.
They sampled different types of tequila during their visit to the distillery in Mexico.
She added a splash of tequila to her homemade salsa for an extra kick of flavor.
The liquor store offered a wide selection of premium tequilas, including blanco, reposado, and añejo varieties.
He learned about the traditional production process of tequila, from harvesting the agave plants to distillation, during his trip to Jalisco.

senses.definition: a strong alcoholic drink made from wine or fruit juice

examples.x: She enjoyed sipping brandy by the fireplace on cold winter evenings.
The cognac, a type of brandy made in the Cognac region of France, was aged for several years in oak barrels.
They shared a bottle of apple brandy they purchased during their visit to the local distillery.
She added a splash of brandy to her hot toddy for a comforting and warming drink.
He learned about the different types of brandy, including Armagnac, Cognac, and Calvados, during a tasting event.

senses.definition: (of a drink) not having bubbles in it
(of drinks) containing bubbles or carbonation

examples.x: She preferred sparkling lemonade over still for its effervescent quality and tangy flavor.

senses.definition: a type of fizzy water that can be mixed with other drinks such as gin or vodka

examples.x: He enjoyed his vodka with a splash of tonic for a light and bubbly drink.
The bar stocked a variety of tonics, from classic to artisanal flavors.
She poured herself a glass of tonic to mix with her gin for a refreshing cocktail.
She preferred tonic with a twist of lemon to complement the botanical notes in her gin.

senses.definition: an alcoholic drink made by mixing several drinks together

examples.x: She enjoyed sipping a classic cocktail like a martini or a mojito on special occasions.
The bartender crafted a signature cocktail for the menu, featuring local spirits and fresh herbs.
They celebrated the end of the week with cocktails at their favorite rooftop bar.
She preferred fruity cocktails with tropical flavors, like a piña colada or a mai tai.
He ordered a fruity cocktail with rum, pineapple juice, and grenadine at the bar.
